symbols: light rays from heaven — divine approval and presence
cross-shaped staff — foreshadowing of the Crucifixion
description: A black-and-white engraving showing Jesus standing in the Jordan River with one arm raised toward heaven. John the Baptist stands beside him holding a cross-topped staff. Dramatic rays of light break through storm clouds. A witness stands to the right observing. The landscape is rendered with Dore's characteristic detailed cross-hatching. Text at bottom references Mark 1.
theological_description: The Baptism of Jesus by John in the Jordan River inaugurates Christ's public ministry. As Jesus rises from the water, the heavens open and the Spirit descends like a dove, while the Father's voice proclaims, 'You are my beloved Son; with you I am well pleased.' This theophany reveals the Trinitarian nature of God. Jesus's willingness to undergo a baptism of repentance, though sinless, demonstrates his solidarity with sinful humanity — he takes upon himself the burden of human sin that he will bear to the cross. John's cross-shaped staff in this engraving foreshadows the Passion to come.

scripture_refs.reason: Mark's account of Jesus's baptism referenced in the image text
Parallel account of the baptism with the heavenly voice
John's testimony: Behold the Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world
